questions.text: I think it is important to remain open and willing to shift with the group.
I can remain focused and clearheaded even in high-pressure situations.
I'd rather enjoy what's available now than wait for something potentially better later.
I believe that building my own capabilities is the foundation of a good life.
I hold the view that emotional detachment during crises is a form of professionalism that is particularly valuable in contexts where multiple lives or livelihoods are at stake.
I rarely look back at past work; each new project is a fresh start.
I prefer to give clear directives rather than open-ended suggestions.
I focus on ideas and content rather than on reading people.
I experience the world at a comfortable sensory and emotional volume.
I experience longing as a physical ache in my chest.
I sense when a conversation has an audience and the speaker is performing.
I am deeply moved by the sight of someone responding to another's distress.
I find that I naturally default to assessing what can be done rather than dwelling on how a situation feels.
I feel most fulfilled when I have given away more than I have kept.
I tend to measure a good day by how much progress I made on my own goals.
I am the kind of person who reads the manual before using a new device.
I prefer working with people who say what they think rather than hinting.
I am the person others call when something needs broad buy-in.
